rationale: The cited paper appears as a single reference in the General Discussion, where the authors note that inserting breaks can reduce hedonic adaptation and that consumers often fail to anticipate this effect. It is used as a related prior example to motivate consumer implications, not to build the focal theory, hypotheses, design, or analyses of the present studies. The core framework in the citing paper centers on similarity, ad hoc categorization, and variety-driven satiation, so this citation functions as contextual background rather than a substantive dependency.
